step_by_step_thinking:
Based on the description provided by agent1 about the immediate binding of antigens to pre-attached antibodies on mast cells or basophils leading to quick symptom onset, it indeed points towards Type I Hypersensitivity. There's no indication that this involves any delayed immune responses characteristic of Types II through IV hypersensitivity reactions. Therefore, my agreement stands firm.

Answer: A Your assessment accurately reflects the characteristics described as fitting into the definition of Type I Hypersensitivity based on its immediate nature involving antigen-antibody interactions triggering mast cell/basophil degranulation swiftly upon re-exposure to allergen.
